content: Raj has ten pairs of red, nine pairs of white and eight pairs of black shoes in a box. If he randomly picks shoes one by one (without replacement) from the box to get a red pair of shoes to wear, what is the maximum number of attempts he has to make?
explanation:
Here's the breakdown of why the answer is Option D, 45:

Raj needs to pick two shoes to make a pair. In the worst-case scenario, he could pick all the shoes that aren't red before finally picking a red pair.

Here's the calculation:

  • Non-red shoes: 9 pairs of white + 8 pairs of black = 17 pairs = 34 shoes

  • Worst-case scenario: Raj picks all 34 non-red shoes, then picks one red shoe to complete a pair.

Therefore, the maximum number of attempts he could make is 34 + 1 = 45.
